content.problemStatement:
Bob is currently at origin. Bob walks on XXX-axis and towards the right. In one move, Bob can either jump 1 unit to the right or 2 units to the right. Some of these coordinates are dirty. Bob wants to know the number of ways going to (Y,0)(Y, 0)(Y,0) from (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0). Two ways are considered to be different if there exists an integer jjj such that the location of Bob after jjj steps is different.

Sounds like a well known problem? Bob quickly came up with a perfect solution.

A valid path should contain '.' which represents a clean coordinate or '#' which represents a dirty coordinate.

If the length of the path is LENLENLEN, then Bob's program calculates and prints number of ways to reach (LEN‚ąí1,0)(LEN-1, 0)(LEN‚ąí1,0) from (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0).

An integer XXX is considered to be a good integer if Bob's program prints XXX for some valid path.

You have to write a program to output YYY, where YYY is NthN^{th}Nth good number in the range LLL to RRR. You should also print the valid path that would make Bob's program output YYY.
If there are multiple valid paths, you can output any of them. Please see the output section for more details.
constraints: Good numbers in range [1,3][1,3][1,3] are 111, 222, 333. 1st1^{st}1st of those is 111. There is one way to reach (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) from (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0).
Good numbers in range [6,9][6,9][6,9] are 666, 888, 999. 3rd3^{rd}3rd of those is 999.
There are only 333 good numbers in range [6,9][6,9][6,9].
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains 3 space separated integers LLL, RRR, and NNN.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output the NthN^{th}Nth good number, followed by space, followed by a valid path. If no such number exists, just output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

A valid path should contain '.' which represent a clean coordinate or '#' which represents a dirty coordinate.

For example, "..##.." represents 666 coordinates, (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ (5.0)(5.0)(5.0) out of which (2,0)(2, 0)(2,0) and (3,0)(3,0)(3,0) are dirty.
